sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To view backup policies, you must have at least the
[Project Read Only](<Project Read Only>) access to the project. Users with
[Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) access must add themselves as a
[Project Read Only](<Project Read Only>) to the project before they can view
backup policies.

To modify or delete backup policies, you must have
[Project Backup Manager](<Project Backup Manager>) or [Project Owner](<Project Owner>)
access to the project. Users with [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>)
access must add themselves as a `Project Backup Manager` or
`Project Owner` to the project before they can modify or delete
backup policies.

sections.heading: View Backup Policies
sections.body:
**Atlascli:**

Atlas CLI

To return the details of the backup policy for the cluster you specify using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas backups schedule describe <clusterName> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas backups schedule describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-backups-schedule-describe).

**Ui:**

Atlas UI

Use the Backup Policy Editor to configure a backup policy for
Cloud Backups.

sections.heading: Step 1: Click .
sections.body:
A backup policy has the following sections:

- A time of day, in UTC (Coordinated Universal Time), at which to create snapshots.

- A frequency interval and duration of retention.

- If PIT (Point in Time) Restores are enabled, a PIT (Point in Time) window that allows you
  to restore to any point in time in the last X days where X is the window.

> **Example:** The default backup policy specifies a snapshot time of `18:00`
> UTC (Coordinated Universal Time) and the following four policy items:
>
> | Policy Type | Tier                                   | Continuous Cloud Backup | Snapshot Taken        | Snapshot Retained |
> | ----------- | -------------------------------------- | ----------------------- | --------------------- | ----------------- |
> | Hourly      | NVMe (non-volatile memory express)     | Enabled                 | Every 12 hours        | 7 days            |
> | Hourly      | non-NVMe (non-volatile memory express) | Enabled                 | Every 6 hours         | 7 days            |
> | Daily       | All                                    | Either                  | Every day             | 7 days            |
> | Weekly      | All                                    | Either                  | Every Saturday        | 4 weeks           |
> | Monthly     | All                                    | Either                  | Last day of the month | 12 months         |
> | Yearly      | All                                    | Either                  | Every 1st of December | 1 year            |

To learn more about Cloud Backup billing, see
[billing](billing-backup-cloud-provider-snapshots).

> **Note:** If you have a [Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy), you
> can't modify the [cluster-level backup policy](configure-backup-policy) for
> an individual cluster below the minimum requirements set in the
> Backup Compliance Policy. You can modify the cluster-level backup policy at any
> time. Atlas augments any preexisting cluster-level policies
> to meet the minimum requirements of the Backup Compliance Policy. All new
> clusters use the Backup Compliance Policy. If you reduce the frequency of a
> backup schedule, the change applies only to future backups. Any
> existing [oplog](manual/reference/glossary) remains for the original window. The minimum
> requirements of the Backup Compliance Policy apply. You can
> [configure extra snapshot retention](config-extra-snapshot-retention). You can also view backup details
> for all `M10+` dedicated clusters including deleted
> clusters with retained snapshots. To learn more, see
> [View Backup Details for Dedicated Clusters](backup-retained-snapshots).

sections.heading: Change the Backup Policy Time
sections.body:
**Atlascli:**

Atlas CLI

To update the backup policy for the cluster you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas backups schedule update [options]

```

To delete the backup policy for the cluster you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas backups schedule delete <clusterName> [options]

```

To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas backups schedule update](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-backups-schedule-update) and
[atlas backups schedule delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-backups-schedule-delete).

Related Links

- [Install the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/install-atlas-cli)

- [Connect to the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli)

**Ui:**

Atlas UI

To modify the backup policy time:

- In the **Backup Policy Editor**, select the hour
  which Atlas takes a snapshot each day from **hr**
  beneath **Snapshot Time (UTC)**.

- Select the number of minutes after **hr** at which
  Atlas takes a snapshot from **min** beneath
  **Snapshot Time (UTC)**.

- Click **Save Changes**.

sections.heading: Configure Your Backup Policy
sections.body:
Each row in the **Backup Policy Frequency and Retention** table
represents a backup policy item. Configure the policy items and,
optionally, add new policy items to configure a new backup policy. See
the [retention considerations](snapshot-retention) before
finalizing your backup policy.

> **Tip:** Atlas displays the estimated number of snapshots
> associated with your changes below the
> **Backup Policy Frequency and Retention** table.

To specify a backup policy item using the Atlas UI:

Select the frequency unit from Frequency Unit
for an existing policy item.

Alternatively, click Add Frequency Unit to add a new
policy item to your backup policy. You can't specify multiple
Hourly or Daily backup policy items.

IMPORTANT: If you have a [Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy), only MongoDB support can delete
[Hourly Snapshot Retention Time](creating-backup-policy) specified in the
Backup Compliance Policy. To delete policy items specified in the Backup Compliance Policy, you
must [MongoDB Support](request-support) and complete an
extensive verification process.

Select the specific value for the frequency unit from
Every.

Specify the retention time for the policy item in
Retention Time and the units for the retention time
from the list the right. To learn more, see
[retention considerations](snapshot-retention).

At the bottom of the page, click Review
Changes to see a summary of differences between your existing
backup policy and your new backup policy.

(Optional) To apply the retention changes in the updated
backup policy to snapshots that Atlas took previously, check
Update retention time of existing
snapshots. You must check this **before** saving your changes.

IMPORTANT: This option affects only snapshots created by
the updated policy items and whose retention has not been
updated individually with the [Update Cloud
Backup Schedule for One Cluster](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updategroupclusterbackupschedule) API call.

(Optional) To delete snapshots and snapshot copies
associated with policies that you delete, check
Delete all snapshots and any associated snapshot
copies from removed policies. You must check this **before**
saving your changes.

Type `delete snapshots` in the prompt that appears when you
check this option.

Click
Confirm.

sections.heading: Configure the Restore Window
sections.body:
> **Note:** If you have a [Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy), only
> MongoDB support can reduce the [Continuous Cloud Backup Restore Window](create-pit-policy). To reduce the Continuous Cloud Backup
> **Restore Window**, the security or legal representative specified for the
> Backup Compliance Policy must [MongoDB Support](request-support) and complete
> an extensive verification process.

You can replay the [oplog](manual/reference/glossary) to restore a cluster from any point in
time within a specified restore window.

To specify the restore window duration, select how long you want
Atlas to retain the oplog for point-in-time restores from
the **Restore Window** list.

You can't configure a restore window that is longer than the
[Hourly Snapshot Retention Time](creating-backup-policy).

sections.heading: Configure Extra Snapshot Retention
sections.body:
When a Backup Compliance Policy is enabled for your project, you can also configure
extra snapshot retention to retain snapshots beyond the Backup Compliance Policy
protection period. Your snapshots remain fully protected and users
can't delete them during the Backup Compliance Policy period. During the extra snapshot
retention period, snapshots are unprotected again and any user with the
appropriate role can delete them. When the extra snapshot retention
period ends, Atlas deletes the snapshots automatically. Any changes
apply to all existing and future snapshots for that frequency unit. The
extra snapshot retention time remains the same even if the Backup Compliance Policy
changes.

To learn more, see [Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy).

To specify extra snapshot retention using the Atlas UI:

In the Extra Snapshot Retention section,
select the frequency unit from the Frequency Unit
dropdown for an existing policy item.

Alternatively, click Add Another Region and
Frequency to add extra snapshot retention for another region.

Select the Retention Time as a combination of
number and interval.

Toggle Point in Time Restore according to
whether or not you want to enable [Continuous Cloud Backup Restore Window](create-pit-policy) for snapshot copies in that region.

At the bottom of the page, click Review
Changes to see a summary of differences between your existing
backup policy and your new backup policy.

Click
Confirm.

sections.heading: Snapshot Retention Considerations
sections.body:
In addition to the **Retention Time** you define for each
Cloud Backup frequency unit, be aware of the following:

- If you delete an existing backup frequency unit, you have the option
  to delete all snapshots created by the policies you delete and all
  associated snapshot copies. To learn more, see [Hourly Snapshot Retention Time](creating-backup-policy).

- Atlas requires the following:

  - The retention time must be equal to or longer than the frequency
    unit. For example, the retention time for weekly snapshot must be
    one week or greater.

  - The value specified for **Retention Time** for items that
    are less frequent must be equal to or larger than the value
    specified for items that are more frequent. For example, if the
    hourly policy item specifies a retention of two weeks, the retention
    for the weekly snapshot must be two weeks or  greater.

- If you have a [Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy),
  you can't decrease the retention time for a snapshot after
  it's taken.

- If overlapping policy items generate the same snapshot, Atlas
  associates the snapshot with the policy item with the longest retention
  time.

  For example, if the policy specifies a daily snapshot with two-day
  retention and a weekly snapshot every Saturday with three-week
  retention, then Atlas displays a frequency of Weekly in
  the Frequency column on the Snapshots page for
  the snapshot taken on Saturday and retains the snapshot for three weeks.
  This is because the weekly policy item has a longer retention time than
  the daily policy item.

- When you disable Cloud Backup or terminate a cluster that has
  Cloud Backup enabled, you can select whether to delete or retain
  existing backup snapshots. To learn more, see
  [Configure Backup Options for the Cluster](create-cluster-backups) and [Terminate active clusters](terminate-cluster).

  For clusters not using [Encryption at Rest using Customer Key Management](security-kms-encryption), you can
  [download the latest snapshot](restore-cloud-provider-snapshot-download) to preserve any data
  stored in the cluster.
